titleZh: AI 日报｜Google、Hugging Face都在把 AI 从能力展示推向真实工作流
titleEn: AI Daily | Where AI capability is turning into workflow
excerptZh: 今天这期 AI 日报有两条并行主线：一条是 前沿研究与能力边界、Agent 与开发者工具，另一条是这些能力正越来越快地落到具体产品和工作流里。Google、Hugging Face、OpenAI 都在用不同方式把这件事往前推…
excerptEn: Today's AI Daily follows two connected threads: Frontier research and capability shifts and Agents and developer tooling…
contentEn:
Today's AI Daily follows two connected threads: Frontier research and capability shifts and Agents and developer tooling, and the speed at which those capabilities are moving into real products and workflows. Google, Hugging Face, OpenAI are all pushing on that arc from different angles.

## Thread 1 | Frontier research and capability shifts

### How AI is helping improve heart health in rural Australia

The headline from Google is not just a fresh announcement, but that Google published a research-led update around dataset, aimed at pushing the capability frontier outward…

The reason this matters is not only the update itself, but how neatly it fits the broader thread: Research-heavy updates do not always become products immediately…

Placed against the last few weeks, the bigger question is whether this moves from a showcase into a default capability. Watch for replications, open implementations, API exposure, or absorption into mainstream products.

Links: [Google AI Blog source](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/health/google-ai-heart-health-australia/) · [RSS feed](https://blog.google/technology/ai/rss/)

---

## Thread 2 | Agents and developer tooling

### How NVIDIA AI-Q Reached \#1 on DeepResearch Bench I and II

The headline from Hugging Face is not just a fresh announcement, but that Hugging Face open-sourced work around context window and open source… One concrete detail from the article stands out…

The reason this matters is not only the update itself, but how neatly it fits the broader thread: The competitive focus is shifting from chat quality to whether models can plug into software, tooling…

Placed against the last few weeks, the bigger question is whether this moves from a showcase into a default capability. Watch whether it gets embedded into real products and developer workflows…

Links: [Hugging Face Blog source](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/how-nvidia-won-deepresearch-bench) · [RSS feed](https://huggingface.co/blog/feed.xml)

---

## Thread 3 | Frontier research and capability shifts

### From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment

The headline from OpenAI is not just a fresh announcement, but that OpenAI pushed research frontier deeper into product and developer workflows… The clearest public signal so far is…

The reason this matters is not only the update itself, but how neatly it fits the broader thread: Research-heavy updates do not always become products immediately…

Placed against the last few weeks, the bigger question is whether this moves from a showcase into a default capability. Watch for replications, open implementations, API exposure, or absorption into mainstream products.

Links: [OpenAI News source](https://openai.com/index/equip-responses-api-computer-environment) · [RSS feed](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## Brief | Agents and developer tooling

### Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection

The headline from OpenAI is not just a fresh announcement, but that OpenAI pushed prompt injection and workflow deeper into product and developer workflows… The clearest public signal so far is…

The reason this matters is not only the update itself, but how neatly it fits the broader thread: The competitive focus is shifting from chat quality to whether models can plug into software, tooling…

Placed against the last few weeks, the bigger question is whether this moves from a showcase into a default capability. Watch whether it gets embedded into real products and developer workflows…

Links: [OpenAI News source](https://openai.com/index/designing-agents-to-resist-prompt-injection) · [RSS feed](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## Brief 5 | Agents and developer tooling

### Rakuten fixes issues twice as fast with Codex

The headline from OpenAI is not just a fresh announcement, but that OpenAI published a hands-on walkthrough around agent workflows and developer tooling… The clearest public signal so far is: Rakuten uses Codex…

The reason this matters is not only the update itself, but how neatly it fits the broader thread: The competitive focus is shifting from chat quality to whether models can plug into software, tooling…

Placed against the last few weeks, the bigger question is whether this moves from a showcase into a default capability. Watch whether it gets embedded into real products and developer workflows…

Links: [OpenAI News source](https://openai.com/index/rakuten) · [RSS feed](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## Brief 6 | Frontier research and capability shifts

### Wayfair boosts catalog accuracy and support speed with OpenAI

The headline from OpenAI is not just a fresh announcement, but that OpenAI published a meaningful update around research frontier. The clearest public signal so far is…

The reason this matters is not only the update itself, but how neatly it fits the broader thread: Research-heavy updates do not always become products immediately…

Placed against the last few weeks, the bigger question is whether this moves from a showcase into a default capability. Watch for replications, open implementations, API exposure, or absorption into mainstream products.

Links: [OpenAI News source](https://openai.com/index/wayfair) · [RSS feed](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)
contentZh:
今天这期 AI 日报有两条并行主线：一条是 前沿研究与能力边界、Agent 与开发者工具，另一条是这些能力正越来越快地落到具体产品和工作流里。Google、Hugging Face、OpenAI 都在用不同方式把这件事往前推。

## 主线一｜前沿研究与能力边界

### Google 把 AI 心血管筛查带到偏远地区

Google这次的更新，核心不是多发了一条新闻，而是 Google公布了一项围绕数据集的研究型更新，目标是推动能力边界继续外扩…

这条消息值得看，不只是因为它本身新，而是因为它和最近的行业主线接得上：研究型更新不一定会马上产品化，但它能提示下一阶段能力边界正在往哪边移动。

如果把它放回这几周的节奏里看，更大的问题是它会不会很快从展示走向默认能力。要看后续是否出现复现、开源实现、API 化，或者被头部产品吸收进默认能力。

相关链接：[Google AI Blog 原文](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/health/google-ai-heart-health-australia/) · [RSS 源](https://blog.google/technology/ai/rss/)

---

## 主线二｜Agent 与开发者工具

### Hugging Face开源上下文窗口、开源

Hugging Face这次的更新，核心不是多发了一条新闻，而是 Hugging Face把围绕上下文窗口、开源的成果进一步开放出来，让外部生态更容易复用、验证和继续搭建…

这条消息值得看，不只是因为它本身新，而是因为它和最近的行业主线接得上：行业竞争点正在从“聊天回答得好不好”转向“能不能稳定接进软件、工具链和自动化流程”，这决定了真正的生产力入口。

如果把它放回这几周的节奏里看，更大的问题是它会不会很快从展示走向默认能力。后续要看它是否被嵌进真实产品和开发者工作流，以及生态是否围绕它形成新的分发与集成方式。

相关链接：[Hugging Face Blog 原文](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/how-nvidia-won-deepresearch-bench) · [RSS 源](https://huggingface.co/blog/feed.xml)

---

## 主线三｜前沿研究与能力边界

### OpenAI推进研究前沿

OpenAI这次的更新，核心不是多发了一条新闻，而是 OpenAI把研究前沿继续往产品和开发者工作流里推进，重点是推动能力边界继续外扩。 目前最明确的公开信号是：How OpenAI built an agent runtime using the Responses API…

这条消息值得看，不只是因为它本身新，而是因为它和最近的行业主线接得上：研究型更新不一定会马上产品化，但它能提示下一阶段能力边界正在往哪边移动。

如果把它放回这几周的节奏里看，更大的问题是它会不会很快从展示走向默认能力。要看后续是否出现复现、开源实现、API 化，或者被头部产品吸收进默认能力。

相关链接：[OpenAI News 原文](https://openai.com/index/equip-responses-api-computer-environment) · [RSS 源](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## 补充｜Agent 与开发者工具

### OpenAI推进提示注入、工作流

OpenAI这次的更新，核心不是多发了一条新闻，而是 OpenAI把提示注入、工作流继续往产品和开发者工作流里推进，重点是把模型能力变成可编排的执行链路…

这条消息值得看，不只是因为它本身新，而是因为它和最近的行业主线接得上：行业竞争点正在从“聊天回答得好不好”转向“能不能稳定接进软件、工具链和自动化流程”，这决定了真正的生产力入口。

如果把它放回这几周的节奏里看，更大的问题是它会不会很快从展示走向默认能力。后续要看它是否被嵌进真实产品和开发者工作流，以及生态是否围绕它形成新的分发与集成方式。

相关链接：[OpenAI News 原文](https://openai.com/index/designing-agents-to-resist-prompt-injection) · [RSS 源](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

OpenAI这次的更新，核心不是多发了一条新闻，而是 OpenAI发布了一篇围绕Agent 工作流与开发工具的实践拆解，重点不是概念，而是它如何进入真实工作流。 目前最明确的公开信号是：Rakuten uses Codex…

## 补充 6｜前沿研究与能力边界

### OpenAI更新研究前沿

OpenAI这次的更新，核心不是多发了一条新闻，而是 OpenAI发布了一条围绕研究前沿的重要更新。 目前最明确的公开信号是：Wayfair uses OpenAI models to improve ecommerce support and product ca…

这条消息值得看，不只是因为它本身新，而是因为它和最近的行业主线接得上：研究型更新不一定会马上产品化，但它能提示下一阶段能力边界正在往哪边移动。

如果把它放回这几周的节奏里看，更大的问题是它会不会很快从展示走向默认能力。要看后续是否出现复现、开源实现、API 化，或者被头部产品吸收进默认能力。

titleZh: 2026-03-11 AI 日报：OpenAI公布指令层级、提示注入
excerptZh: 这期只保留近 24 小时里最值得继续跟进的 6 条 AI 更新，主线集中在 AI 产品体验、检索、多模态与记忆系统、模型安全与可控性。其中 2 条补抓了原文正文，所以解释不只停留在 RSS 片段。
excerptEn: This issue keeps only the 6 highest-signal AI updates from the last 24 hours, concentrated around AI product experience, Retrieval, multimodal, and memory systems, Model safety and controllability. 2 of them include fetched full article bodies, so the commentary goes beyond RSS snippets.
contentEn:
> Method: RSS candidates first, then full-body fetches for high-value items, then local rule-based bilingual briefing generation. If full-body fetch fails, the issue says so explicitly.

This issue keeps only the 6 highest-signal AI updates from the last 24 hours, concentrated around AI product experience, Retrieval, multimodal, and memory systems, Model safety and controllability. 2 of them include fetched full article bodies, so the commentary goes beyond RSS snippets.

## 01 · Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs

**At a glance**
OpenAI published a research-driven update around instruction hierarchy and prompt injection, aimed at making models more controllable under conflicting instructions. The strongest signal in the article is: IH-Challenge trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving instruction hierarchy, safety steerability…

**Why it matters**
- What changed: OpenAI published a research-driven update around instruction hierarchy and prompt injection, aimed at making models more controllable under conflicting instructions.
- Why it matters: This kind of progress changes whether frontier models can obey higher-priority instructions reliably in production, which directly affects enterprise adoption, controllable agents, and high-risk deployments.
- What to watch: Watch whether it moves into APIs, system-prompt policy controls, and public eval baselines instead of remaining a research-only result.

**Evidence**
- IH-Challenge trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving instruction hierarchy, safety steerability, and resistance to prompt injection attacks.

**Source note**
- Built from RSS snippet and title only

**Links**
- [OpenAI News source](https://openai.com/index/instruction-hierarchy-challenge)
- [RSS feed](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## 02 · New ways to learn math and science in ChatGPT

**At a glance**
OpenAI rolled out a new move around interactive visual explanations and math and science, extending its push on turning capability into repeatable product behavior. The strongest signal in the article is: ChatGPT introduces interactive visual explanations for math and science, helping students explore formulas, variables, and concepts in real time…

**Why it matters**
- What changed: OpenAI rolled out a new move around interactive visual explanations and math and science, extending its push on turning capability into repeatable product behavior.
- Why it matters: Once capability lands inside everyday user actions, competition shifts from raw model scores to depth of use, retention, interaction design, and default entry points.
- What to watch: The next thing to watch is user behavior change: more frequent opens, new default habits, and measurable gains in retention or monetization.

**Evidence**
- ChatGPT introduces interactive visual explanations for math and science, helping students explore formulas, variables, and concepts in real time.

**Source note**
- Built from RSS snippet and title only

**Links**
- [OpenAI News source](https://openai.com/index/new-ways-to-learn-math-and-science-in-chatgpt)
- [RSS feed](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## 03 · How NVIDIA Builds Open Data for AI

**At a glance**
Hugging Face pushed embedding model and multimodal deeper into product and developer workflows, with a clear emphasis on bringing more unstructured data into the same retrieval space. The strongest signal in the article is: Enterprise + Article Published March 10, 2026 - A collaborative approach to scaling trustworthy AI systems and agents. Why it matters…

**Why it matters**
- What changed: Hugging Face pushed embedding model and multimodal deeper into product and developer workflows, with a clear emphasis on bringing more unstructured data into the same retrieval space.
- Why it matters: These lower-layer capabilities determine the quality ceiling for search, recommendations, knowledge systems, and multimodal retrieval—many AI products become meaningfully better or worse here.
- What to watch: Watch whether quality, cost, and tooling support create a real gap—and whether enterprise knowledge bases, RAG stacks, and cross-modal search move quickly in response.

**Evidence**
- Enterprise + Article Published March 10, 2026 - A collaborative approach to scaling trustworthy AI systems and agents.
- The datasets also supported the development of NVIDIA Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2-Japanese , a state-of-the-art sub-10B model that reached the top of the Nejumi leaderboard .
- In-domain fine-tuning of embedding models demonstrates substantial gains: fine-tuning nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 on this dataset yields an 11% increase in NDCG@10.

**Source note**
- Full article body fetched (~1498 words)

**Links**
- [Hugging Face Blog source](https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/open-data-for-ai)
- [RSS feed](https://huggingface.co/blog/feed.xml)

---

## 04 · Gemini in Google Sheets just achieved state-of-the-art performance.

**At a glance**
Google pushed google sheets and benchmark deeper into product and developer workflows, with a clear emphasis on turning capability into repeatable product behavior. The strongest signal in the article is: Today we announced new beta features for Gemini in Sheets to help you create, organize and edit entire sheets… Why it matters…

**Why it matters**
- What changed: Google pushed google sheets and benchmark deeper into product and developer workflows, with a clear emphasis on turning capability into repeatable product behavior.
- Why it matters: Once capability lands inside everyday user actions, competition shifts from raw model scores to depth of use, retention, interaction design, and default entry points.
- What to watch: The next thing to watch is user behavior change: more frequent opens, new default habits, and measurable gains in retention or monetization.

**Evidence**
- Today we announced new beta features for Gemini in Sheets to help you create, organize and edit entire sheets…
- Achieving a 70.48% success rate, Gemini in Sheets not only exceeds competitors but nears human expert ability.
- Gemini in Sheets has reached state-of-the-art proficiency in autonomously manipulating complex, real-world spreadsheets on the full SpreadsheetBench dataset…

**Source note**
- Full article body fetched (~121 words)

**Links**
- [Google AI Blog source](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/workspace/gemini-google-sheets-state-of-the-art/)
- [RSS feed](https://blog.google/technology/ai/rss/)

---

## 05 · The era of “AI as text” is over. Execution is the new interface.

**At a glance**
GitHub pushed agentic workflows and copilot sdk deeper into product and developer workflows, with a clear emphasis on turning model capability into orchestrated execution workflows. The strongest signal in the article is: AI is shifting from prompt-response interactions to programmable execution. Why it matters…

**Why it matters**
- What changed: GitHub pushed agentic workflows and copilot sdk deeper into product and developer workflows, with a clear emphasis on turning model capability into orchestrated execution workflows.
- Why it matters: The competitive focus is shifting from chat quality to whether models can plug into software, tooling, and automation flows reliably; that is where durable productivity entry points emerge.
- What to watch: Watch whether it gets embedded into real products and developer workflows, and whether the ecosystem reorganizes around new distribution and integration patterns.

**Evidence**
- AI is shifting from prompt-response interactions to programmable execution.
- See how the GitHub Copilot SDK enables agentic workflows directly inside your applications.
- The post The era of “AI as text” is over.

**Source note**
- Built from RSS snippet and title only

**Links**
- [GitHub Blog / AI & ML source](https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/the-era-of-ai-as-text-is-over-execution-is-the-new-interface/)
- [RSS feed](https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/feed)

---

## 06 · Google AI Introduces Gemini Embedding 2: A Multimodal Embedding Model that Lets Your Bring Text, Images, Video, Audio, and Docs into the Embedding Space

**At a glance**
Google published a research-driven update around multimodal embedding and embedding model, aimed at bringing more unstructured data into the same retrieval space. The strongest signal in the article is: Google expanded its Gemini model family with the release of Gemini Embedding 2. Why it matters…

**Why it matters**
- What changed: Google published a research-driven update around multimodal embedding and embedding model, aimed at bringing more unstructured data into the same retrieval space.
- Why it matters: These lower-layer capabilities determine the quality ceiling for search, recommendations, knowledge systems, and multimodal retrieval—many AI products become meaningfully better or worse here.
- What to watch: Watch whether quality, cost, and tooling support create a real gap—and whether enterprise knowledge bases, RAG stacks, and cross-modal search move quickly in response.

**Evidence**
- Google expanded its Gemini model family with the release of Gemini Embedding 2.
- This second-generation model succeeds the text-only gemini-embedding-001 and is designed specifically to address the high-dimensional storage and cross-modal retrieval challenges f…
- The Gemini Embedding 2 release marks a significant technical shift…

**Source note**
- Built from RSS snippet and title only

**Links**
- [MarkTechPost source](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/03/11/google-ai-introduces-gemini-embedding-2-a-multimodal-embedding-model-that-lets-your-bring-text-images-video-audio-and-docs-into-the-embedding-space/)
- [RSS feed](https://www.marktechpost.com/feed/)
contentZh:
> 方法：先抓 RSS 候选，再对高价值条目优先抓原文正文，最后用本地规则生成中英双语 briefing；如果正文抓取失败，会明确说明。

这期只保留近 24 小时里最值得继续跟进的 6 条 AI 更新，主线集中在 AI 产品体验、检索、多模态与记忆系统、模型安全与可控性。其中 2 条补抓了原文正文，所以解释不只停留在 RSS 片段。

## 01 · OpenAI公布指令层级、提示注入

**一句话判断**
OpenAI公布了一项围绕指令层级、提示注入的研究型更新，目标是让模型在复杂提示冲突里更可控。 正文里最值得记下的信号是：IH-Challenge trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving 指令层级, 安全 steerability, and resistance to 提示注入 attacks…

**为什么值得跟进**
- 发生了什么：OpenAI公布了一项围绕指令层级、提示注入的研究型更新，目标是让模型在复杂提示冲突里更可控。
- 为什么重要：这类进展会直接影响模型在真实产品里能否稳定遵循高优先级指令，是企业接入、Agent 可控性和高风险场景落地的基础。
- 接下来观察：下一步要看它会不会进入 API、系统提示策略和公开评测基线，而不只是停留在研究展示。

**正文证据**
- IH-Challenge trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving 指令层级, 安全 steerability, and resistance to 提示注入 attacks.

**来源说明**
- 仅基于 RSS 摘要与标题

**相关链接**
- [OpenAI News 原文](https://openai.com/index/instruction-hierarchy-challenge)
- [RSS 源](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## 02 · OpenAI发布交互式可视化讲解、数学与科学

**一句话判断**
OpenAI发布了一个围绕交互式可视化讲解、数学与科学的新动作，继续沿着“把 AI 能力做成可重复使用的产品体验”这条线推进。 正文里最值得记下的信号是：ChatGPT 发布 交互式可视化讲解 用于 数学与科学, helping students explore formulas, variables, and concepts in real time. 为什么值得看：当能力真正进入用户日常动作时…

**为什么值得跟进**
- 发生了什么：OpenAI发布了一个围绕交互式可视化讲解、数学与科学的新动作，继续沿着“把 AI 能力做成可重复使用的产品体验”这条线推进。
- 为什么重要：当能力真正进入用户日常动作时，竞争会从模型分数转向使用深度、留存、交互设计和默认入口。
- 接下来观察：后续重点是看用户行为是否改变：是否更频繁打开、是否形成新的工作习惯、是否带来付费或留存提升。

**正文证据**
- ChatGPT 发布 交互式可视化讲解 用于 数学与科学, helping students explore formulas, variables, and concepts in real time.

**来源说明**
- 仅基于 RSS 摘要与标题

**相关链接**
- [OpenAI News 原文](https://openai.com/index/new-ways-to-learn-math-and-science-in-chatgpt)
- [RSS 源](https://openai.com/news/rss.xml)

---

## 03 · Hugging Face推进嵌入模型、多模态

**一句话判断**
Hugging Face把嵌入模型、多模态进一步推进到产品和开发者工作流里，重点放在提升非结构化信息进入同一检索空间的能力。 正文里最值得记下的信号是：Enterprise + Article Published March 10, 2026 - A collaborative approach to scaling trustworthy AI systems and agents…

**为什么值得跟进**
- 发生了什么：Hugging Face把嵌入模型、多模态进一步推进到产品和开发者工作流里，重点放在提升非结构化信息进入同一检索空间的能力。
- 为什么重要：这类底层能力决定搜索、推荐、知识库和多模态检索系统的可用性，很多 AI 产品体验的上限取决于这里。
- 接下来观察：接下来要看效果、成本和工具链支持是否拉开差距，以及企业知识库、RAG 和跨模态搜索是否快速跟进。

titleZh: OpenClaw 终极安装指南：从零搭建你的私有 AI 助手
titleEn: The Ultimate OpenClaw Setup Guide: Build Your Private AI Assistant
excerptZh: 完整记录 OpenClaw 的安装配置流程，涵盖 Telegram、Discord 接入、技能扩展与常见问题排查，帮你少踩坑。
excerptEn: A complete walkthrough of OpenClaw installation and configuration, covering Telegram/Discord integration, skill extensions, and common troubleshooting.
contentEn:
OpenClaw (formerly known as Clawdbot) is an open-source project that lets you run a private AI assistant on your own computer. It integrates with WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and other major messaging platforms, supports executing local commands, remembers user preferences, and keeps all data local—nothing goes through third-party servers.

After spending some time getting it set up, I've compiled the complete installation and configuration process into this guide to help you avoid common pitfalls.

---

## Prerequisites

Before getting started, make sure you have the following:

### 1. Operating System

Supports macOS, Windows, and Linux—all three platforms work.

### 2. Node.js (Version 22 or Higher)

OpenClaw is built with JavaScript, so you need the Node.js runtime.

**Check if already installed:**

```bash
node --version
```

If you see something like `v22.1.0`, you're good. If you get "command not found" or a version below 22, you'll need to install it first.

### 3. AI Service Account

OpenClaw doesn't include an AI model—it connects to third-party AI services. Currently supported:

- **Anthropic (Claude)**: Recommended; Claude Opus 4.5 works great
- **OpenAI (ChatGPT)**: Also supported; works with GPT-4 series

---

## Step 1: Install OpenClaw

Open Terminal (macOS/Linux) or PowerShell (Windows) and run the installation command:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash
```

Verify the installation afterward:

```bash
openclaw --version
```

---

## Wrapping Up

Overall, the OpenClaw installation process isn't particularly complex—most of the time is spent understanding the components and configuring messaging channels. Once it's running and connected to your daily Telegram or Discord, the experience is quite solid.

If you run into issues during installation, feel free to leave a comment below, or check the [official documentation](https://docs.openclaw.ai).
contentZh:
OpenClaw（前身叫 Clawdbot）是一个让你在自己电脑上运行私有 AI 助手的开源项目。它可以接入 WhatsApp、Telegram、Discord、Slack 等主流消息平台，支持执行本地命令、记忆用户偏好，并且数据完全本地化——不经过任何第三方服务器。

折腾了一段时间之后，我把完整的安装和配置流程整理成了这篇笔记，希望能帮你少踩一些坑。

---

## 准备工作

在开始之前，需要确认以下几件事：

### 1. Node.js（版本 22 或更高）

OpenClaw 基于 JavaScript 构建，所以需要 Node.js 运行环境。

```bash
node --version
```

---

## 总结

整体来说，OpenClaw 的安装流程并不复杂，主要的时间花在理解各个组件的作用和配置消息渠道上。一旦跑起来，把它接入日常使用的 Telegram 或 Discord，体验还是相当不错的。

titleZh: 2026 春节档 AI 大乱斗：Qwen3.5、MiniMax M2.5、GLM-5、Kimi K2.5 深度对比，以及我对 OpenClaw 层级价值的判断
titleEn: Spring 2026 Open-Source Model Showdown: Qwen3.5, MiniMax M2.5, GLM-5, Kimi K2.5 — And Why the Orchestration Layer Matters
excerptZh: 四个模型的技术路线各有侧重，但有一个共同方向：把工具调用和 Agent 能力放在比"聪明程度"更优先的位置。这背后，是 OpenClaw 这个层级正在崛起的价值。
excerptEn: Four models, four technical approaches, one shared direction: prioritizing tool-calling and agent capabilities over raw intelligence. Behind this trend lies the rising value of the orchestration layer.
contentEn:
In the week surrounding Chinese New Year 2026, China's AI scene put on a collective release show: Qwen3.5, MiniMax M2.5, GLM-5, and Kimi K2.5 all dropped within days of each other. I've been putting each of them through their paces, and the more I use them, the more I notice something interesting—their technical approaches differ, but they share a common direction: **prioritizing tool-calling and agent capabilities over raw intelligence**.

This post is my research notes on these four models, ending with a broader observation I've been sitting with.

---

## Qwen3.5: Native Multimodal Agent on a Hybrid Architecture

Alibaba's Qwen3.5 is the most architecturally adventurous of the bunch. The flagship Qwen3.5-397B-A17B uses a **hybrid architecture fusing linear attention (via Gated Delta Networks) with sparse mixture-of-experts (Sparse MoE)**—397B total parameters, but only 17B activated per forward pass. This achieves top-tier capability while dramatically reducing inference costs.

The language coverage expansion is also notable: from 119 languages in the previous generation to **201 languages and dialects**, which has real implications for global deployment.

**On tool calling**, Qwen3.5 performs impressively. It scores 72.9 on BFCL-V4 (tool use benchmark) and 46.1 on MCP-Mark (MCP protocol tool calling), both placing it in the top tier among open-source models. The hosted Qwen3.5-Plus comes with built-in official tools, adaptive tool selection, and a 1 million token context window.

The **small-scale versions** are equally interesting: Qwen3.5-9B outperforms OpenAI's gpt-oss-120B on multiple benchmarks while running on a standard laptop—a significant development for locally deployed agent frameworks.

| Capability | Performance |
|-----------|-------------|
| Architecture | Linear attention + Sparse MoE hybrid |
| Active parameters | 17B (of 397B total) |
| Tool calling | BFCL-V4: 72.9, MCP-Mark: 46.1 |
| Context window | 1M tokens (Plus version) |
| Language support | 201 languages and dialects |
| Open source | Yes (Qwen3.5-397B-A17B) |

---

## My Take: The Value of the OpenClaw Layer

After spending time with these models, I keep coming back to the same observation.

The current AI tool usage pattern looks roughly like this: you open Claude or ChatGPT, describe your need, wait for an answer, then go execute it yourself. The model is the "consultant," you are the "executor."

But tools like OpenClaw represent a different paradigm: **the model itself is the executor**. It doesn't just give you suggestions—it calls tools, runs commands, manages files, sends messages. You describe the goal; it completes the task.

There's a key cognitive shift here: **the execution-layer model doesn't need to be the smartest—it just needs to call tools accurately and orchestrate task flows correctly**.

*These are my thoughts from recent experimentation—not necessarily right, happy to discuss.*
contentZh:
2026 年的春节档，中国 AI 圈上演了一场集体发布秀：Qwen3.5、MiniMax M2.5、GLM-5、Kimi K2.5 在短短一周内相继亮相。我把这几个模型都折腾了一遍，越用越觉得有意思——它们的技术路线各有侧重，但有一个共同的方向：**把工具调用和 Agent 能力放在了比"聪明程度"更优先的位置**。

这篇文章是我的调研笔记，最后会聊聊我从中得出的一个判断。

---

## 我的判断：OpenClaw 这个层级的价值

用了这几个模型一段时间之后，我有一个越来越强烈的感受。

现在的 AI 工具使用范式，大致是这样的：你打开 Claude 或 ChatGPT，描述你的需求，等它给你答案，然后你再去执行。模型是"顾问"，你是"执行者"。

但 OpenClaw 这类工具代表的是另一种范式：**模型本身就是执行者**。它不只是给你建议，而是直接调用工具、执行命令、管理文件、发送消息。你描述目标，它完成任务。

*以上是我最近折腾这几个模型的一些想法，不一定对，欢迎讨论。*
